title: Quiz: Temporal Information Processing in QRC
description: Test your understanding of temporal processing in quantum reservoir computing

questions.question: What does quantum memory capacity measure in a reservoir?

options.text: The number of qubits in the system
The ability to linearly recover past inputs from current features
The total entanglement in the system

questions.explanation: Quantum memory capacity measures how well past inputs u(t-k) can be linearly reconstructed from current observable measurements x(t). It quantifies the reservoir's ability to retain temporal information.

questions.question: What is the theoretical upper bound for total memory capacity in a reservoir?

options.text: The dimension of the Hilbert space

questions.explanation: Total memory capacity is bounded by the number of linearly independent features that can be measured from the reservoir. For N qubits with 3N single-qubit Pauli observables, this gives an upper bound of 3N.

questions.question: In the Mackey-Glass time series, what parameter value leads to chaotic dynamics?
questions.explanation: The Mackey-Glass system exhibits chaotic behavior when the delay parameter τ > 17. This makes it a standard benchmark for testing reservoir computing performance on chaotic time series.

questions.question: What are the two sources of fading memory in quantum reservoirs?
questions.explanation: Quantum fading memory arises from (1) decoherence processes that naturally damp quantum coherence, and (2) unitary evolution that mixes information throughout the Hilbert space, gradually obscuring past inputs.

questions.question: Quantum reservoirs always have better memory capacity than classical reservoirs of the same size.
questions.explanation: False. While quantum reservoirs can access an exponentially large Hilbert space, the measurable features are limited to polynomial-scaling observables. The advantage depends on the specific task and Hamiltonian design.

questions.question: In sequence classification with quantum reservoirs, what features are typically used?

options.text: Features from only the first time step
Average of all time step features

questions.explanation: For sequence classification, the final quantum state encodes information about the entire sequence. Observable measurements of this final state provide features that summarize the sequence for classification.

questions.question: What does nonlinear processing capacity measure?

options.text: The ability to compute nonlinear functions of past inputs
The training time of the readout layer

questions.explanation: Nonlinear processing capacity measures the reservoir's ability to compute and represent nonlinear functions of past inputs, such as u(t-1)², or cross-terms like u(t-1)×u(t-2).

questions.question: Which techniques are used to train the output layer in temporal quantum reservoir computing? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The output layer in reservoir computing (both classical and quantum) is trained using linear methods: ridge regression (linear least squares with regularization) or standard linear least squares. The reservoir itself is not trained.
